You are a senior game designer + Godot engineer reviewing an existing small game called "Glamdring".

FIRST, read these files to fully understand the game (use Read):
- C:\Users\duran\Documents\godot\glamdring\CLAUDE.md  (project overview + conventions)
- C:\Users\duran\Documents\godot\glamdring\gameplay.md  (authoritative rules, FSM, timings)
- C:\Users\duran\Documents\godot\glamdring\plan.md  (architecture, tunable constants, layout)
- C:\Users\duran\Documents\godot\glamdring\balance.json  (runtime tunables)
- C:\Users\duran\Documents\godot\glamdring\fight.gd  (tick loop, swing resolves)
- C:\Users\duran\Documents\godot\glamdring\player.gd  (player FSM)
- C:\Users\duran\Documents\godot\glamdring\enemy.gd + C:\Users\duran\Documents\godot\glamdring\enemy_basic.gd  (enemy FSM)
- C:\Users\duran\Documents\godot\glamdring\combatant.gd  (base class)
- C:\Users\duran\Documents\godot\glamdring\hud.gd
- C:\Users\duran\Documents\godot\glamdring\sprites\SPRITES.md  (sprite cell layout)

THE GAME: 1v1 reaction combat in Godot 4.6. Fixed 16 ticks/sec timestep. Player (left) is INVINCIBLE in v1; goal is to drop enemy HP to 0. Enemy has an FSM: idle -> threat_weak/threat_strong (telegraph) -> recovery -> idle, with an exhausted side-branch when fatigue hits 0. Player has weak attack, strong attack, block (with an 8-tick "active parry" window that drains enemy fatigue), and dodge. The core loop is "read the telegraph, find the opening": you can only damage the enemy during its recovery/exhausted windows; hitting it while idle does nothing (it blocks), and getting hit REGENERATES the enemy (passivity is punished).

HARD ARCHITECTURE CONSTRAINTS (a proposal that violates these is bad):
- NO physics, NO Area2D, NO CollisionShape — everything is stateful, manual.
- All gameplay logic runs strictly in _on_tick() at 16 Hz. Rendering via queue_redraw().
- Gameplay tunables go in balance.json (loaded by the Balance autoload). Visual/animation constants stay as const in scripts.
- Sprites are a horizontal strip, one cell per FSM stance, swapped via region_rect — NO inter-pose animation, NO AnimationPlayer. Player sheet = 7 cells, enemy_basic = 6 cells. Adding a brand-new visual stance ideally REUSES an existing cell or a modulate tint (the user would have to draw new art otherwise — call this out).
- Changes to rules/timing MUST be sync-able to gameplay.md + plan.md (note this, don't write them).

YOUR LENS: JUICE & GAME FEEL — features that make every hit and moment satisfying within a sprite-swap + draw_rect engine: hit-stop/freeze-frames, screen flash, slow-mo, dynamic camera zoom/shake, afterimages, particles, chromatic punch, sound-cue hooks. Pure feel, minimal new rules, maximal "wow".

Through THIS lens, propose a COHESIVE package of 2-4 new features that would make the game genuinely more interesting/fun WITHOUT breaking the careful existing design. Each feature must be concrete and tied to the real code: name the exact files, FSM states, and balance.json keys you'd add or change, with specific tick timings/values. Prefer features that need no new sprite art (reuse cells / tints / pure fx); if a feature needs art, say so and give an art-free fallback.

Be bold and specific — the user explicitly asked to be surprised. But every idea must be implementable in this tick-based, no-physics, sprite-swap architecture. Self-assess feasibility honestly.

1	# CLAUDE.md — Glamdring
2	
3	Reaction-combat 1v1 hra v Godot 4.6 Mobile profilu. Player (stance sprite vlevo, zelené tóny) vs Enemy (stance sprite vpravo, šedo-červené tóny). Pevný timestep **16 ticků/s**.
4	Pro implementace pouzivej sub agenty at je hlavni context cistsi.
5	
6	## Autoritativní specifikace
7	
8	- **`gameplay.md`** — pravidla, FSM stavy, timingy, balance, swing resolve.
9	- **`plan.md`** — architektura souborů, tunable konstanty, stance sprite rendering, vizuální layout, verifikační scénáře.
10	- **`sprites/SPRITES.md`** — per-character spritesheet layout (cell index → stance).
11	
12	Před netriviální změnou si přečti oba soubory. Pokud upravuješ pravidla/timing, **synchronizuj s gameplay.md i plan.md** (linter to nedělá).
13	
14	## Project structure
15	
16	```
17	fight.tscn / fight.gd        — kořenová scéna + tick loop, swing resolves, HUD update, win check
18	combatant.gd                 — class_name Combatant (base: hp, fatigue, state, state_ticks_remaining)
19	enemy.gd                     — class_name Enemy extends Combatant (FSM transitions, abstract hooks)
20	enemy_basic.gd               — extends Enemy (per-type tunable konstanty)
21	player.gd                    — extends Combatant (input → state transitions)
22	hud.gd                       — CanvasLayer (HP/fatigue bary, debug state text, win overlay)
23	balance.json                 — runtime tunables (HP/fatigue caps, ticky, damage, cooldowns, AI prob.)
24	balance.gd                   — autoload singleton `Balance`, načítá balance.json při startu
25	sprites/                     — stance spritesheets (`player.png`, `enemy_basic.png`) + `SPRITES.md`
26	project.godot                — fight.tscn jako main_scene, 720×1280 portrait, stretch=viewport,
27	                               autoload Balance="*res://balance.gd"
28	```
29	
30	## Conventions
31	
32	- **Tick loop:** akumulátor v `fight.gd._process(delta)`; veškerá herní logika (state countdowny, AI, swing resolve, hit resolve) **výhradně** v `_on_tick()`. Vykreslování přes `queue_redraw()` na konci `_process`.
33	- **Tunable konstanty:** Herní balanc (ticky, damage, cooldowns, HP/fatigue caps, AI probabilities) je v `balance.json` v rootu projektu, načítaný autoloadem `Balance` při startu. Editace JSONu + restart aplikuje změny bez recompilu. Vizuální / animační / architektonické konstanty (`TICKS_PER_SECOND`, `SPRITE_SHEET`, `SPRITE_CELL_SIZE`, `SPRITE_SCALE`, `SPRITE_INDEX`, HP/fatigue bar dimensions, shake/particle hodnoty) zůstávají jako `const` v příslušném skriptu. Per-type AI tunables enemy (`threat_*_ticks`, `recovery_*_ticks`, `idle_*`, `strong_probability`) jsou v sekci `enemy_basic` JSONu.
34	- **Per-type overrides:** `enemy.gd` poskytuje defaults pro `_threat_*_ticks` apod. `enemy_basic.gd` je přepisuje a vrací hodnoty z `Balance.*`. Při ladění hodnot **edituj `balance.json`**, base `enemy.gd` zůstává s hardcoded defaults pro hypotetické budoucí typy.
35	- **No physics:** žádné `PhysicsBody`, `Area2D`, `CollisionShape`. Vše stavové; pokud potřeba kolize, ručně AABB/distance check.
36	- **Stance sprites:** Combatant = body `Sprite2D` + weapon `Sprite2D` overlay (oba s `region_rect` cell-pickerem nad horizontálním sheetem). Weapon sheet má identický layout jako body, takže index se počítá jednou a aplikuje na oba sprity. Každý FSM stance = jedna buňka. `SPRITE_INDEX` dict v každém `.gd` mapuje stance → cell index. Sub-stance (player active/passive block, strong windup/recovery) řeší `_current_visual_stance()`. Žádné inter-pose animace — pouze sprite swap na hranici ticku. Hit flash = `modulate * 2.5` na obě vrstvy. Spec stancí + sheet layouty viz `sprites/SPRITES.md`.
37	- **HUD constants:** bary používají `Balance.hp_max` / `Balance.fatigue_max` pro percenta — pokud změníš cap v `balance.json`, bar se škáluje automaticky.
38	- **Czech:** uživatel píše česky, technické termíny v EN. Komentáře v kódu mohou být kombinované (jak existuje).
39	
40	## Controls (v1 — keyboard only)
41	
42	| Klávesa | Akce |
43	|---|---|
44	| `→` (tap) | Fast weak attack (instant swing, 4t recovery, 12t cooldown, damage 15) |
45	| `↑` | Strong attack (6-tick telegraph + 10-tick recovery) |
46	| `↓` (hold) | Block — prvních 8t "active parry" (drain enemy fatigue při weak útoku) |
47	| `←` (tap) | Dodge (6t i-frames + 2t recover, 24t cooldown) |
48	| `Space` | Restart po výhře (mimo gameplay) |
49	
50	Touch input pro mobil je odložený TODO (viz plan.md "Mimo scope").
51	
52	## How to verify
53	
54	Otevři projekt v Godot 4.6 editoru, spusť `fight.tscn`. Manuální test scénáře v `plan.md` sekce **Verifikace** — projdi 1–9.
55	
56	## Working style
57	
58	- Když uživatel přidá / změní gameplay pravidlo: vždy aktualizuj **kód v glamdring** + **gameplay.md** + **plan.md** současně.
59	- Drobné implementační detaily (vizuál, barvy, anchor pozice) ne nutně do md — patří jen pokud mění herní pocit nebo design intent.
60	- Před commitem zkontroluj že md a kód jsou konzistentní (typicky: hodnoty v `balance.json`, klávesy, damage hodnoty).
61	- Glamdring **není git repo** (zatím) — uživatel commituje ručně nebo si ho založí.
62	
63	## Out of scope (v1)
64	
65	- Touch input pro mobil.
66	- Více typů nepřátel (base `Enemy` připravena, jen `enemy_basic` v této iteraci).
67	- Player stats / smrt (player invincible).
68	- Zvuky, hudba, particle efekty, screenshake.
69	- Sword sprity / AnimationPlayer (modulární API to umožní bez refaktoru).
70	- Persistence skóre, save game, menu / scene flow.
71

1	# Gameplay: Reakční souboj 1v1 (Fight Scene)
2	
3	> Popis pravidel a herní mechaniky. Implementační detaily viz `plan.md`.
4	
5	## Přehled
6	
7	1v1 reakční souboj. Vlevo zelený "player", vpravo bílý "enemy". Hra běží na pevném timestepu **16 ticků/s** — všechny časové parametry jsou v ticích. Mobilní portrétová orientace.
8	
9	**Cíl:** Dostat enemy `hp` na 0. Player v1 nesmrtelný. Pasivita je penalizována (zásah hráče regeneruje nepřítele).
10	
11	## Tick systém
12	
13	Veškerá herní logika běží na 16 Hz (≈62.5 ms/tick). Z toho se odvozují všechny délky stavů, telegrafů, útoků a cooldownů. Vykreslování běží na frame-rate displeje.
14	
15	## Enemy
16	
17	### Stats
18	- `hp = 100` (cap 100, min 0)
19	- `fatigue = 40` (cap 40, min 0)
20	
21	### Stavy
22	
23	| Stav | Délka (ticky) | Popis |
24	|---|---|---|
25	| `idle` | 32–64 (random) | Čeká mezi útoky, blokuje útoky hráče |
26	| `threat_weak` | 24 | Meč napřažen pro slabý útok |
27	| `threat_strong` | 32 | Meč napřažen pro silný útok (vizuálně: enemy obarven červeně) |
28	| `recovery_weak` | 8 | Recovery po slabém seku, zranitelný |
29	| `recovery_strong` | 16 | Recovery po silném seku, zranitelný |
30	| `exhausted` | 64 | `fatigue == 0`; neútočí, neblokuje, všechny útoky zasáhnou |
31	
32	### Přechody
33	
34	```
35	idle (po vypršení) ──► decide attack:
36	    ├─ 80 %: threat_weak  (24t) ──► [swing] ──► recovery_weak  (8t)  ──► idle
37	    └─ 20 %: threat_strong (32t) ──► [swing] ──► recovery_strong (16t) ──► idle
38	
39	fatigue → 0 (kdykoli) ──► exhausted (64t) ──► idle (fatigue := 40)
40	```
41	
42	`[swing]` je instant 0-tick event na posledním ticku threat fáze.
43	
44	### Reakce v `idle` na útok hráče
45	
46	| Útok hráče | Enemy efekt |
47	|---|---|
48	| weak | žádný (zablokuje) |
49	| strong | `fatigue −= 10` (zablokuje se ztrátou) |
50	
51	Vizuální feedback: v obou případech enemy svým mečem **vykryje** (krátký flinch nahoru a zpět) — hráč tak vidí, že swing nedopadl, místo aby zíral na statického enemy.
52	
53	### Reakce v `recovery_*` / `exhausted` na útok hráče
54	
55	| Útok hráče | Efekt |
56	|---|---|
57	| weak | `enemy.hp −= 15` |
58	| strong | `enemy.hp −= 25` |
59	
60	### Reakce v `threat_*` na útok hráče
61	
62	Žádný efekt. Telegraph nelze přerušit.
63	
64	## Player
65	
66	### Stats (v1)
67	Žádné. Player je invincible.
68	
69	### Stavy
70	
71	| Stav | Délka (ticky) | Popis |
72	|---|---|---|
73	| `idle` | — | Přijímá input |
74	| `attacking_weak` | 4 (instant swing + 4t recover), pak 12t cooldown | Rychlý slabý útok — žádný telegraph, swing na 1. ticku. Cooldown zahrnuje recovery, takže net čekání po návratu do `idle` je ~8t (0.5 s). |
75	| `attacking_strong` | 16 (6 windup + 10 recover) | Silný útok — krátký telegraph 6 ticků |
76	| `blocking` | dokud držíš `↓` | Obrana — prvních 8 ticků "active parry" (drainuje enemy fatigue při weak útoku), dále jen pasivní (chrání, ale fatigue nedrainuje). Re-press resetuje window. |
77	| `dodging` | 8 (6 invincibility + 2 recover), pak 24t cooldown | Úhyb |
78	
79	Během `attacking_*` a `dodging` jsou ostatní vstupy ignorovány. `blocking` opustíš puštěním `↓`. Weak cooldown blokuje jen další `→` — block / dodge / strong jsou v jeho průběhu volné.
80	
81	### Klávesy
82	
83	Šipky (dpad-style):
84	
85	- `→` (vpravo, tap) — fast weak attack (instant swing + 4t recovery)
86	- `↑` (nahoru) — strong attack
87	- `↓` (dolů, hold) — block
88	- `←` (vlevo, tap) — dodge
89	
90	`Space` (mimo gameplay) restartuje scénu po výhře.
91	
92	### Swing event hráče
93	
94	- **weak:** Po stisku `→` swing event okamžitě (0-tick windup), hráč přejde do 4-tickového `attacking_weak` recovery, pak ~8t v idle s aktivním cooldownem (celkem 12t od stisku). Fixní damage 15 HP. Reakční nástroj na enemy recovery / exhausted; cooldown drží svižnost tím, že brání spam-mashi.
95	
96	- **strong:** Swing event na **6. ticku** stavu `attacking_strong` (recovery 7.–16.). Fixní damage 25 HP.
97	
98	> Charging weak attack varianta (drž `→` pro nabíjení 1–15 HP) byla prototypovaná v commitu `317b1d2` a později reverted. Archiv designu + kódu: `features/charge.md`.
99	
100	V obou případech se efekt aplikuje na enemy na základě jeho aktuálního stavu (viz Enemy tabulky výše) a současně se spustí swing animace.
101	
102	## Swing resolve (na posledním ticku enemy `threat_*`)
103	
104	V pořadí:
105	
106	1. `player.state == dodging` → **miss**. Žádný efekt.
107	2. Slabý útok (`threat_weak`) a `player.state == blocking`:
108	   - **Active parry** (block započal v posledních 8 tickách): `enemy.fatigue −= 10`. Žádný hit.
109	   - **Pasivní block** (block drží déle než 8 ticků): hráč chráněn, ale žádný drain. Žádný hit.
110	3. Silný útok (`threat_strong`) a `player.state == blocking` → **blok neúčinný** (fall-through na 4).
111	4. Jinak → **hráč zasažen**:
112	   - `enemy.hp = min(100, enemy.hp + 10)`
113	   - `enemy.fatigue = min(40, enemy.fatigue + 30)`
114	
115	Filozofie: zásah hráče **regeneruje** nepřítele — player je invincible, takže pasivita musí být penalizována.
116	
117	## Win condition
118	
119	- `enemy.hp <= 0` → "VÝHRA" overlay. `Space` restartuje scénu.
120	- Lose condition v1 neexistuje.
121	
122	## Souhrn FSM diagramů
123	
124	### Enemy
125	```
126	                  ┌── 80 % ──► threat_weak (24t) ──[swing]──► recovery_weak  (8t)  ──┐
127	idle (32–64t) ───►│                                                                 │
128	                  └── 20 % ──► threat_strong (32t) ─[swing]──► recovery_strong (16t) ──┤
129	                                                                                     ▼
130	                                          ┌──────────────────────────────────────  idle (loop)
131	fatigue → 0 ───► exhausted (64t) ──► idle (fatigue=40)
132	```
133	
134	### Player
135	```
136	idle ──┬─ → tap ──► attacking_weak (0t windup + 4t recovery, swing instant) ──► idle
137	       ├─ ↑     ──► attacking_strong (16t, swing na 6. ticku) ──► idle
138	       ├─ ↓ ↓   ──► blocking ─(↓ ↑)─► idle
139	       └─ ← tap ──► dodging (8t) ──► idle  (+ 24t cooldown na další dodge)
140	```
141	
142	## Co tento gameplay v1 nepokrývá
143	
144	- Touch input pro mobil — v1 jen klávesnice.
145	- Více typů nepřátel — v1 jen jeden základní vzor (32–64 idle, 20 % strong).
146	- Player stats (HP, stamina) — v1 invincible.
147	- Postup / level progression — v1 jediný souboj, restart po výhře.
148

1	# Implementační plán: Fight Scene
2	
3	> Technický plán implementace. Herní pravidla, FSM, timingy a balance viz `gameplay.md`.
4	
5	## Cílové prostředí
6	
7	- Godot **4.x** (vyvíjeno na 4.6 mobile profil; bez fyziky, bez collision shapes — vše manuálně).
8	- Viewport **720×1280** portrait. `window/stretch/mode="viewport"` doporučeno.
9	- Vstup v1: **klávesnice — šipky** (`→` weak, `↑` strong, `↓` block-hold, `←` dodge; `Space` restart po výhře). Touch input je odložený TODO.
10	- Žádné `PhysicsBody`, žádné `Area2D` — kolize/hit checks čistě stavové.
11	
12	## Tick smyčka
13	
14	Pevný timestep 16 Hz akumulátorem v `_process`:
15	
16	```gdscript
17	const TICKS_PER_SECOND := 16
18	const TICK_DURATION := 1.0 / TICKS_PER_SECOND
19	var tick_acc := 0.0
20	
21	func _process(delta: float) -> void:
22	    tick_acc += delta
23	    while tick_acc >= TICK_DURATION:
24	        tick_acc -= TICK_DURATION
25	        _on_tick()
26	    queue_redraw()
27	```
28	
29	V `_on_tick()`:
30	```
31	player.tick()              # state countdown, dodge cooldown
32	enemy.tick()               # state countdown
33	if enemy.swing_this_tick:  # poslední tick threat fáze
34	    _resolve_enemy_swing()
35	if player.swing_this_tick: # poslední tick windupu attacking_*
36	    _resolve_player_swing()
37	hud.update(...)
38	```
39	
40	Pokud framerate klesne pod 16fps, while-loop dohoní ticky. State countdowns, AI rozhodnutí, swing resolve i hit resolve běží **výhradně** v `_on_tick()`.
41	
42	## Architektura souborů
43	
44	```
45	fight.tscn             # kořenová scéna (Node2D), instancuje Player + Enemy + HUD
46	fight.gd               # tick loop, swing/hit resolve, HUD update, win check
47	
48	combatant.gd           # class_name Combatant extends Node2D
49	                       #   hp, fatigue, state, state_ticks_remaining
50	                       #   set_state(name, ticks), tick()
51	
52	enemy.gd               # class_name Enemy extends Combatant
53	                       #   abstract: _pick_attack(rng) -> StringName ("weak"|"strong")
54	                       #   abstract: _pick_idle_duration(rng) -> int
55	
56	enemy_basic.gd         # extends Enemy — první typ
57	                       #   _pick_attack: 20 % strong / 80 % weak
58	                       #   _pick_idle_duration: randi_range(32, 64)
59	
60	player.gd              # extends Combatant
61	                       #   input → state transitions
62	                       #   dodge_cooldown_ticks
63	
64	sprites/               # spritesheety + dokumentace
65	  player.png                 #   body, 224×32, 7 cells of 32×32 (stance per cell)
66	  player_weapon.png          #   weapon overlay, identický layout jako body
67	  enemy_basic.png            #   body, 192×32, 6 cells of 32×32
68	  enemy_basic_weapon.png     #   weapon overlay, identický layout jako body
69	  SPRITES.md                 #   per-cell stance spec + extension guide
70	
71	hud.gd                 # CanvasLayer
72	                       #   update(enemy_hp, enemy_fatigue, enemy_state, enemy_state_ticks,
73	                       #          player_state, player_state_ticks_or_active_block,
74	                       #          dodge_cd, show_win)
75	                       #   bary nad enemy se kreslí v enemy.gd, HUD je už jen debug + win overlay.
76	```
77	
78	`fight.tscn` jako main scéna (`run/main_scene="res://fight.tscn"` v `project.godot`).
79	
80	Třída `Combatant` je base pro `Player` i `Enemy`, drží společná pole (`hp`, `fatigue`, `state`, `state_ticks_remaining`). `Enemy` přidává abstract hooky pro per-type chování — `enemy_basic.gd` je první konkrétní implementace, další typy se přidají jako nové podtřídy `Enemy` bez dotyku base classy.
81	
82	## Tunable konstanty
83	
84	Tyto hodnoty jsou v `balance.json` a načítají se autoload singletonem `Balance` při startu. Tabulky níže slouží jako reference defaultů; reálné runtime hodnoty ber z JSONu. Kód odkazuje na `Balance.<field>` (např. `Balance.hp_damage_weak`).
85	
86	Vizuální / animační konstanty (`TICKS_PER_SECOND`, `SPRITE_*`, bar dimensions, shake/particle hodnoty) zůstávají v `const` blocích na vrcholu příslušných skriptů.
87	
88	**`balance.json`** (defaults mirrored from below):
89	
90	```json
91	{
92	  "combatant": { "hp_max": 100, "fatigue_max": 40 },
93	  "player": {
94	    "attack_weak_windup": 0, "attack_weak_recover": 4, "attack_weak_cooldown": 12,
95	    "attack_strong_windup": 6, "attack_strong_recover": 10,
96	    "dodge_invincibility": 6, "dodge_recover": 2, "dodge_cooldown": 24,
97	    "block_active_ticks": 8
98	  },
99	  "enemy_basic": {
100	    "threat_weak_ticks": 8, "threat_strong_ticks": 16,
101	    "recovery_weak_ticks": 8, "recovery_strong_ticks": 16,
102	    "exhausted_ticks": 64,
103	    "idle_min_ticks": 32, "idle_max_ticks": 64,
104	    "strong_probability": 0.20
105	  },
106	  "fight": {
107	    "hp_damage_weak": 15, "hp_damage_strong": 25,
108	    "enemy_fatigue_blocked_weak": 10, "enemy_fatigue_player_strong_idle": 10,
109	    "enemy_hp_regen_on_hit": 10, "enemy_fatigue_regen_on_hit": 30,
110	    "hit_flash_ticks": 3
111	  }
112	}
113	```
114	
115	**`enemy_basic`** (per-enemy-type AI tunables) — viz `enemy_basic.gd`, vše čte z `Balance.*`:
116	- `threat_weak_ticks` / `threat_strong_ticks` — délka napřahu weak / strong (ticky)
117	- `recovery_weak_ticks` / `recovery_strong_ticks` — délka recovery po seku
118	- `exhausted_ticks` — doba exhausted state (po vyčerpání fatigue)
119	- `idle_min_ticks` / `idle_max_ticks` — rozsah random idle pauzy
120	- `strong_probability` — pravděpodobnost strong útoku (0.20 = 20 %)
121	
122	**`player`** — viz `player.gd`:
123	- `attack_weak_windup = 0` (weak je instant — swing fires na stisk)
124	- `attack_weak_recover = 4`, `attack_weak_cooldown = 12` (cooldown počítá i recovery; net čekání po idle ≈ 8t)
125	- `attack_strong_windup = 6` (strong má krátký telegraph 6 ticků), `attack_strong_recover = 10`
126	- `dodge_invincibility = 6`, `dodge_recover = 2`, `dodge_cooldown = 24`
127	- `block_active_ticks = 8` (první N ticků blocking jsou "active parry" — drain fatigue)
128	
129	**`fight`** (resolve numbers) — viz `fight.gd`:
130	- `hp_damage_weak = 15`, `hp_damage_strong = 25`
131	- `enemy_fatigue_blocked_weak = 10`, `enemy_fatigue_player_strong_idle = 10`
132	- `enemy_hp_regen_on_hit = 10`, `enemy_fatigue_regen_on_hit = 30`
133	- `hit_flash_ticks = 3`
134	
135	**`combatant`** — viz `combatant.gd`:
136	- `hp_max = 100`, `fatigue_max = 40` (caps pro `clampi` + HUD percenta v `enemy.gd`)
137	
138	Pokud `balance.json` chybí / je rozbitý, `balance.gd._load_balance()` zaloguje `push_error` a ponechá defaults v deklaracích `var` (kód se nezhroutí).
139	
140	> Charging weak varianta (drž `→` pro 1–15 damage) je archivovaná v `features/charge.md` pro pozdější návrat.
141	
142	## Stance sprite rendering
143	
144	Combatant = dva `Sprite2D` child nody (body + weapon overlay), oba s `region_rect` cell-pickerem nad horizontálními spritesheety. Žádné inter-pose animace, žádný `AnimationPlayer`, žádné lerpy — každý FSM stance má **právě jednu** buňku v sheetu a změna stancu = swap `region_rect` na hranici ticku. Body sprite je `add_child`ován jako první → kreslí se pod weapon spritem (Godot child draw order).
145	
146	**Layout:**
147	- Body sheet: `res://sprites/<character>.png` (horizontální strip).
148	- Weapon sheet: `res://sprites/<character>_weapon.png` (**identický layout** — stejný count cell, stejný cell size, stejné pořadí stancí). Index se počítá jednou a aplikuje na oba sprity současně.
149	- Cell size je per-character const v `.gd` (`SPRITE_CELL_SIZE`):
150	  - `player.png` / `player_weapon.png` — 224×32, 7 cells of 32×32
151	  - `enemy_basic.png` / `enemy_basic_weapon.png` — 192×32, 6 cells of 32×32
152	- Display scale ×8 (`SPRITE_SCALE`) s `texture_filter = NEAREST` pro pixel-perfect rendering — 32px cell → 256px na obrazovce. Hit flash modulate se aplikuje na obě vrstvy.
153	
154	**Mapování stance → cell:**
155	`SPRITE_INDEX := { &"<stance>": <idx>, ... }` v každém combatant `.gd`. `_update_sprite()` se volá na konci `tick()` (a okamžitě po `set_state(...)` ve vstupních handlerech v `player.gd`), nastaví `region_rect` a `modulate`.
156	
157	**Sub-stance (jen player):**
158	`_current_visual_stance()` odvozuje vizuální stance z FSM stavu + sub-state proměnných:
159	- `attacking_strong` + `windup_ticks_remaining > 0` → `attacking_strong_windup`
160	- `attacking_strong` + `windup_ticks_remaining == 0` → `attacking_strong_recovery`
161	- `blocking` + `is_active_block()` → `blocking_active`
162	- `blocking` + jinak → `blocking_passive`
163	
164	Enemy nemá sub-stance — mapování `state -> idx` je 1:1.
165	
166	**Hit flash:** `sprite.modulate = Color(2.5, 2.5, 2.5, 1.0)` po dobu `hit_flash_ticks > 0`, jinak `Color.WHITE`. Žádný separate flash sprite cell.
167	
168	**Per-character cell spec a extension guide:** viz `sprites/SPRITES.md`.
169	
170	## Vizuální layout (720×1280)
171	
172	```
173	+------------------------+
174	|     [Background.png]   |  ← horní 2/5 viewportu (0..512), CanvasLayer layer=-1
175	|     (scale ×2, anchor) |     (568×440 centrovaný horizontálně, bottom @ y=512)
176	|                        |
177	|                        |
178	|              [HP]      |  ← floating bars nad enemy (drawn v enemy.gd):
179	|              [Fat]     |     HP 120×10 @ y_off=−150, Fatigue 120×6 @ y_off=−138
180	|   [PLAYER]   [ENEMY]   |  ← y=384 (sprite bottom @ 3/5 z dolního okraje), scaled ×8 (~256 px)
181	|   (stance)   (stance)  |    player.x=264, enemy.x=456 (~8 src-px overlap při scale ×8)
182	|                        |
183	|                        |
184	|                        |
185	|  ↑=strong  →=weak      |  ← y≈1200, debug hint text
186	|  ↓=block   ←=dodge     |
187	+------------------------+
188	```
189	
190	**Background** (`sprites/Background.png`, 284×206 src-px) je v `fight.tscn` jako `Sprite2D` uvnitř `CanvasLayer` s `layer = -1`, čímž se renderuje pod combatants i HUDem. `texture_filter = NEAREST`, integer scale ×6 → 1704×1236 px (přesahuje viewport nahoře i po stranách, záměrně). Bottom-anchored na "podlahu" y=512 (kde stojí postavy), position = `(360, -106)`. Pokud změníš výšku bg textury (cropnutím/přidáním), drž `position.y = 512 - (height * scale / 2)` aby bottom seděl s nohama postav. **Screen shake:** Fight.gd mirruje `position` (Node2D shake offset) do `background_canvas.offset` v `_update_shake`, takže bg se třese společně s ostatními combatants.
191	
192	Pozice jsou vodítka — finální doladit po hraní. Sprite je child node combatantu se default position (0,0) — pohyb combatantu (shake) ho táhne s sebou.
193	
194	**HP + fatigue bary enemy** jsou drawn v `enemy.gd._draw()` jako floating bary nad tělem (HP 120×10 px na y_off=−82, fatigue 120×6 px na y_off=−70 od středu). Důsledek: bary se hýbou se screen shake (parent `Fight` Node2D má shake offset → enemy + bary se posunou společně) a hit flash na ně nepůsobí (bary jsou samostatné draw cally po body rectu, ne tělo). `Combatant.damage_hp/heal_hp/add_fatigue/drain_fatigue` volají `queue_redraw()` aby se bar refreshly mimo state-transition ticky. HUD je už jen debug text + win overlay.
195	
196	Vizuální stav indikace:
197	- Stance sprite swap (jedna buňka per stance) — viz `sprites/SPRITES.md`.
198	- **Polish vrstva** (`fight.gd`, čistě prezentační — gameplay neovlivňuje):
199	  - Screen shake při hitech, amplitude per-event (weak / strong / fatigue). Decay 30 px/s.
200	  - Hit flash: zasažený combatant 3 ticky bílý (`hit_flash_ticks` v `Combatant` se propaguje jako `sprite.modulate = Color(2.5, 2.5, 2.5, 1.0)` v `_update_sprite()`).
201	  - Particles: malé čtverečky odlétávají z bodu zásahu (oranžové = HP damage, žluté = fatigue drain). Per-frame update v `_process`, life ~0.45 s, alpha fade, drag 0.92. Spawn pouze na tick boundaries v resolve funkcích.
202	
203	## Verifikace (manuální testy)
204	
205	Po implementaci spustit `fight.tscn` a projít:
206	
207	1. **Idle baseline:** nedělej nic 30 s → enemy útočí pravidelně, `hp` na cap 100, `fatigue` na cap 40. HP bar plný, fatigue bar plný.
208	2. **Active parry weak:** stiskni `↓` těsně před weak telegraph → enemy.fatigue −= 10 (25 % bar). Mimo 8-tick window → pouze ochrání, žádný drain.
209	3. **Blok strong neúčinný:** drž `↓` před strong telegraph (červený enemy) → enemy získá +10 HP / +30 fatigue. (Pokud jsou na capu, zůstanou na capu — pro test sniž je nejdřív hitem.)
210	4. **Dodge:** stiskni `←` ve správný čas → miss, žádný efekt. Druhý `←` během 24-tick cooldown nesmí zafungovat.
211	5. **Strong v idle:** stiskni `↑` během enemy idle → `enemy.fatigue −= 10`. Opakuj 4× → enemy přejde do `exhausted` na 64 ticků (vizuálně šedý).
212	6. **Weak v recovery:** stiskni `→` po enemy seku → `enemy.hp −= 15`. Swing je instant (žádný windup).
213	7. **Strong v exhausted:** během `exhausted` stiskni `↑` → `enemy.hp −= 25`.
214	8. **Win:** doved enemy HP na 0 → "VÝHRA" overlay, `Space` zavolá `reload_current_scene()`.
215	9. **Tunable:** změň jednu konstantu (např. `THREAT_WEAK_TICKS := 12` v `enemy_basic.gd`) a ověř, že timing slabého napřahu se zrychlí.
216	
217	## Mimo scope této iterace (technické)
218	
219	- Reálný stance art (zatím jen barevné dummy buňky); skutečný pixel-art vyplní stejné 24×N buňky.
220	- Inter-pose animace (multi-frame sprites na stance, `AnimationPlayer`).
221	- Touch input pro mobil — UI vrstva pro dotyková tlačítka, gesture detection.
222	- Další podtřídy `Enemy` (base připravena, ale jen `enemy_basic` v této iteraci).
223	- Player stats (HP, stamina) — `Combatant` to umožní bez refaktoru.
224	- Zvuky, hudba, particle efekty, screenshake.
225	- Persistence skóre, save game.
226	- Menu / scene flow vně `fight.tscn`.

1	extends Node2D
2	
3	# Fight scene controller. Owns the 16 Hz tick accumulator, calls combatant
4	# ticks in order, resolves swing events, updates the HUD, checks win condition.
5	
6	const TICKS_PER_SECOND := 16
7	const TICK_DURATION := 1.0 / TICKS_PER_SECOND
8	
9	# Gameplay resolve numbers (damage, fatigue, regen, hit flash) live in
10	# `balance.json` and are accessed via the `Balance` autoload. Only visual
11	# polish constants (shake/particles) remain as `const` below.
12	
13	# Polish fx — purely visual, no gameplay impact.
14	const SHAKE_HIT_WEAK := 4.0
15	const SHAKE_HIT_STRONG := 10.0
16	const SHAKE_FATIGUE := 3.0
17	const SHAKE_DECAY_PER_SEC := 30.0
18	const PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE := 10
19	const PARTICLES_FATIGUE := 6
20	const PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS := 0.45
21	const PARTICLE_SPEED_MIN := 80.0
22	const PARTICLE_SPEED_MAX := 220.0
23	const PARTICLE_SIZE := 4.0
24	const PARTICLE_DRAG := 0.92  # velocity multiplier per frame at 60 fps reference
25	const COLOR_HIT_DAMAGE := Color(1.0, 0.5, 0.3, 1.0)
26	const COLOR_FATIGUE_FX := Color(0.95, 0.85, 0.3, 1.0)
27	const COLOR_PLAYER_HIT := Color(0.9, 0.3, 0.3, 1.0)
28	
29	@onready var player: Node2D = $Player
30	@onready var enemy: Node2D = $Enemy
31	@onready var hud: CanvasLayer = $HUD
32	@onready var background_canvas: CanvasLayer = $Background
33	
34	var tick_acc: float = 0.0
35	var game_over: bool = false
36	
37	# Polish fx state.
38	var shake_amount: float = 0.0
39	var rng_fx := RandomNumberGenerator.new()
40	# Each particle: {pos: Vector2, vel: Vector2, life: float, color: Color}
41	var particles: Array = []
42	
43	
44	func _process(delta: float) -> void:
45		tick_acc += delta
46		while tick_acc >= TICK_DURATION:
47			tick_acc -= TICK_DURATION
48			_on_tick()
49		_update_shake(delta)
50		_update_particles(delta)
51		queue_redraw()
52	
53	
54	func _update_shake(delta: float) -> void:
55		if shake_amount <= 0.0:
56			position = Vector2.ZERO
57			background_canvas.offset = Vector2.ZERO
58			return
59		var dir := Vector2(
60			rng_fx.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0),
61			rng_fx.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0)
62		)
63		if dir.length() > 0.001:
64			dir = dir.normalized()
65		position = dir * shake_amount
66		# Mirror shake into the background CanvasLayer (it's outside the Fight
67		# Node2D transform, so offset is the only way to shake it).
68		background_canvas.offset = position
69		shake_amount = maxf(0.0, shake_amount - SHAKE_DECAY_PER_SEC * delta)
70	
71	
72	func _update_particles(delta: float) -> void:
73		for i in range(particles.size() - 1, -1, -1):
74			var p: Dictionary = particles[i]
75			p.life -= delta
76			if p.life <= 0.0:
77				particles.remove_at(i)
78				continue
79			p.pos += p.vel * delta
80			p.vel *= PARTICLE_DRAG
81	
82	
83	func _trigger_shake(amount: float) -> void:
84		# Combine — don't downgrade an already-bigger shake in progress.
85		shake_amount = maxf(shake_amount, amount)
86	
87	
88	func _spawn_particles(at: Vector2, color: Color, count: int) -> void:
89		for _i in count:
90			var angle := rng_fx.randf_range(-PI, PI)
91			var speed := rng_fx.randf_range(PARTICLE_SPEED_MIN, PARTICLE_SPEED_MAX)
92			var vel := Vector2(cos(angle), sin(angle)) * speed
93			particles.append({
94				"pos": at,
95				"vel": vel,
96				"life": PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS,
97				"color": color,
98			})
99	
100	
101	func _between_combatants() -> Vector2:
102		return (player.position + enemy.position) * 0.5
103	
104	
105	func _draw() -> void:
106		for p: Dictionary in particles:
107			var alpha := clampf(p.life / PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS, 0.0, 1.0)
108			var col: Color = p.color
109			col.a = alpha
110			var s := PARTICLE_SIZE
111			draw_rect(Rect2(p.pos - Vector2(s, s) * 0.5, Vector2(s, s)), col)
112	
113	
114	func _on_tick() -> void:
115		if game_over:
116			# In win state — only listen for restart.
117			if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_SPACE):
118				get_tree().reload_current_scene()
119			_update_hud()
120			return
121	
122		# Tick order: player first, enemy second. (Doesn't materially matter because
123		# resolution happens after both have ticked, but keeps deterministic.)
124		player.tick()
125		enemy.tick()
126	
127		# Player input handled at tick boundary (post-countdown so freshly-finished
128		# states allow new input on the same tick).
129		player.handle_input()
130	
131		# Resolve enemy swing first (enemy attack lands on player).
132		if enemy.swing_this_tick:
133			_resolve_enemy_swing()
134	
135		# Then player swing (player attack lands on enemy).
136		if player.swing_this_tick:
137			_resolve_player_swing()
138	
139		# Fatigue → exhausted transition (enemy only — player has no fatigue).
140		if enemy.fatigue <= 0 and enemy.state != &"exhausted":
141			enemy.enter_exhausted()
142	
143		# Win check.
144		if enemy.hp <= 0:
145			game_over = true
146	
147		_update_hud()
148	
149	
150	# --- Resolves ------------------------------------------------------------
151	
152	func _resolve_enemy_swing() -> void:
153		# Enemy swing event on the last tick of threat_*.
154		var kind: StringName = enemy.pending_attack_kind
155	
156		# 1. Player dodging (invincible window) → miss.
157		if player.state == &"dodging" and player.dodge_invincible:
158			return
159	
160		# 2. Weak attack + player blocking → block. Fatigue drain ONLY if the
161		# block is still in its active parry window; otherwise it just protects.
162		if kind == &"weak" and player.state == &"blocking":
163			if player.is_active_block():
164				enemy.drain_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_blocked_weak)
165				_trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
166				_spawn_particles(_between_combatants(), COLOR_FATIGUE_FX, PARTICLES_FATIGUE)
167			return
168	
169		# 3. Strong attack + player blocking → block ineffective (fall through).
170		# 4. Otherwise → player hit (player invincible, but enemy regens).
171		enemy.heal_hp(Balance.enemy_hp_regen_on_hit)
172		enemy.add_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_regen_on_hit)
173		_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_WEAK if kind == &"weak" else SHAKE_HIT_STRONG)
174		player.trigger_flash(Balance.hit_flash_ticks)
175		_spawn_particles(player.position, COLOR_PLAYER_HIT, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
176	
177	
178	func _resolve_player_swing() -> void:
179		var kind: StringName = player.pending_attack_kind
180		var es: StringName = enemy.state
181	
182		# Enemy in threat_* → no effect (telegraph cannot be interrupted).
183		if es == &"threat_weak" or es == &"threat_strong":
184			return
185	
186		# Enemy in idle → blocks player attacks, with strong draining fatigue.
187		if es == &"idle":
188			if kind == &"strong":
189				enemy.drain_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_player_strong_idle)
190				_trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
191				_spawn_particles(enemy.position, COLOR_FATIGUE_FX, PARTICLES_FATIGUE)
192			# TODO: future visual block feedback could be a temporary sprite swap
193			# (e.g. a `block_react` stance cell) on enemy.gd. For now no extra fx.
194			return
195	
196		# Enemy in recovery_* or exhausted → hit lands.
197		if es == &"recovery_weak" or es == &"recovery_strong" or es == &"exhausted":
198			if kind == &"weak":
199				enemy.damage_hp(Balance.hp_damage_weak)
200				_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_WEAK)
201			else:
202				enemy.damage_hp(Balance.hp_damage_strong)
203				_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_STRONG)
204			enemy.trigger_flash(Balance.hit_flash_ticks)
205			_spawn_particles(enemy.position, COLOR_HIT_DAMAGE, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
206			return
207	
208		# Fallback (shouldn't normally hit): treat as blocked.
209	
210	
211	func _update_hud() -> void:
212		# State-ticks field is repurposed per-state for visibility:
213		#   blocking → active parry window remaining (0..Balance.block_active_ticks)
214		#   other    → state_ticks_remaining
215		var p_ticks: int = player.state_ticks_remaining
216		if player.state == &"blocking":
217			p_ticks = player.active_block_ticks_remaining()
218		hud.update(
219			enemy.hp, enemy.fatigue, enemy.state, enemy.state_ticks_remaining,
220			player.state, p_ticks,
221			player.dodge_cooldown_ticks, game_over
222		)
223

1	extends Combatant
2	
3	# Player FSM. Input drives state transitions. Swing event lands on the last
4	# tick of the windup; fight.gd reads `swing_this_tick` + `pending_attack_kind`.
5	
6	# Gameplay tunables (timing, cooldowns, parry window) live in `balance.json`
7	# and are accessed via the `Balance` autoload. Only visual / layout constants
8	# remain as `const` below.
9	
10	# --- Sprite rendering ----------------------------------------------------
11	# Player body = Sprite2D with region_rect cell-picker. Each FSM stance
12	# (including derived sub-stances) maps to exactly one cell in the strip.
13	# A second Sprite2D (weapon) overlays the body using the same cell index —
14	# weapon sheet has the same layout, just a separate texture.
15	# See sprites/SPRITES.md for the per-cell stance spec.
16	const SPRITE_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/player.png")
17	const WEAPON_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/player_weapon.png")
18	const SPRITE_CELL_SIZE := Vector2i(32, 32)
19	const SPRITE_SCALE := 8.0
20	const SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE := Color(2.5, 2.5, 2.5, 1.0)
21	const SPRITE_INDEX := {
22		&"idle": 0,
23		&"attacking_weak": 1,
24		&"attacking_strong_windup": 2,
25		&"attacking_strong_recovery": 3,
26		&"blocking_active": 4,
27		&"blocking_passive": 5,
28		&"dodging": 6,
29	}
30	
31	var sprite: Sprite2D
32	var weapon_sprite: Sprite2D
33	
34	# Set true for exactly one tick when player executes a swing event.
35	var swing_this_tick: bool = false
36	# &"weak" or &"strong" — read on swing tick.
37	var pending_attack_kind: StringName = &"weak"
38	
39	# Remaining ticks of the windup portion of an attack. When this hits 0 and
40	# state is attacking_*, swing fires on that tick and recovery begins.
41	var windup_ticks_remaining: int = 0
42	
43	# Ticks elapsed since entering &"blocking". Used to compute active parry window.
44	var blocking_ticks_elapsed: int = 0
45	
46	# Tracks dodge cooldown (separate from state countdown so we can be in idle
47	# while still on cooldown).
48	var dodge_cooldown_ticks: int = 0
49	
50	# Tracks weak-attack cooldown (counted alongside the attacking_weak state — by
51	# the time we're back in idle, this still has a few ticks left to gate spam).
52	var weak_cooldown_ticks: int = 0
53	
54	# True while we're in the invincibility portion of dodging.
55	var dodge_invincible: bool = false
56	
57	
58	func _ready() -> void:
59		sprite = _make_sprite(SPRITE_SHEET)
60		add_child(sprite)
61		# weapon_sprite added after body → drawn on top in same _draw pass.
62		weapon_sprite = _make_sprite(WEAPON_SHEET)
63		add_child(weapon_sprite)
64		set_state(&"idle", 0)
65		_update_sprite()
66	
67	
68	func _make_sprite(sheet: Texture2D) -> Sprite2D:
69		var s := Sprite2D.new()
70		s.texture = sheet
71		s.region_enabled = true
72		s.region_rect = Rect2(0, 0, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.y)
73		s.scale = Vector2(SPRITE_SCALE, SPRITE_SCALE)
74		s.texture_filter = CanvasItem.TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST
75		return s
76	
77	
78	# Called by fight.gd in tick order BEFORE input handling each tick so countdown
79	# advances first. Input handling lives separately in handle_input().
80	func tick() -> void:
81		swing_this_tick = false
82		if dodge_cooldown_ticks > 0:
83			dodge_cooldown_ticks -= 1
84		if weak_cooldown_ticks > 0:
85			weak_cooldown_ticks -= 1
86	
87		# Attack states use a separate windup counter so we can fire the swing
88		# event on the last windup tick, then continue into recovery within the
89		# same `attacking_*` state.
90		if state == &"attacking_weak" or state == &"attacking_strong":
91			if windup_ticks_remaining > 0:
92				windup_ticks_remaining -= 1
93				if windup_ticks_remaining == 0:
94					# This tick IS the last windup tick — swing now.
95					swing_this_tick = true
96	
97		# Track block age for parry window — only the first block_active_ticks of a
98		# block drain enemy fatigue.
99		if state == &"blocking":
100			blocking_ticks_elapsed += 1
101	
102		# Update dodge phase tracking before generic countdown.
103		if state == &"dodging":
104			# Invincibility lasts the first dodge_invincibility ticks of the
105			# 8-tick state. state_ticks_remaining starts at 8 and decrements.
106			var elapsed: int = Balance.dodge_invincibility + Balance.dodge_recover - state_ticks_remaining
107			dodge_invincible = elapsed < Balance.dodge_invincibility
108	
109		super.tick()
110	
111		if state_expired_this_tick:
112			_on_state_expired()
113	
114		_update_sprite()
115	
116	
117	func _on_state_expired() -> void:
118		match state:
119			&"attacking_weak", &"attacking_strong", &"dodging":
120				dodge_invincible = false
121				set_state(&"idle", 0)
122			_:
123				pass
124	
125	
126	# Called from fight.gd._on_tick() AFTER tick() so input is sampled at tick
127	# boundaries (not per-frame). This keeps everything deterministic on the tick
128	# grid.
129	func handle_input() -> void:
130		# Inputs are only accepted in idle or blocking states. attacking_* and
131		# dodging swallow input until they finish.
132		# v1 keyboard-only mapping: arrow keys. Right = fast weak attack, Up =
133		# strong attack, Down (hold) = block, Left (tap) = dodge.
134		match state:
135			&"idle":
136				# Block has highest priority (held) — must check before tap actions
137				# so player can transition out of attacks-finished into block on
138				# the same tick.
139				if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_DOWN):
140					set_state(&"blocking", 0)
141					blocking_ticks_elapsed = 0
142					_update_sprite()
143					return
144				if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_RIGHT) and weak_cooldown_ticks == 0:
145					_begin_attack(&"weak")
146					return
147				if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_UP):
148					_begin_attack(&"strong")
149					return
150				if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_LEFT) and dodge_cooldown_ticks == 0:
151					_begin_dodge()
152					return
153			&"blocking":
154				if not Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_DOWN):
155					set_state(&"idle", 0)
156					_update_sprite()
157	
158	
159	func _begin_attack(kind: StringName) -> void:
160		pending_attack_kind = kind
161		var windup: int
162		var recover: int
163		if kind == &"weak":
164			windup = Balance.attack_weak_windup
165			recover = Balance.attack_weak_recover
166			set_state(&"attacking_weak", windup + recover)
167			weak_cooldown_ticks = Balance.attack_weak_cooldown
168		else:
169			windup = Balance.attack_strong_windup
170			recover = Balance.attack_strong_recover
171			set_state(&"attacking_strong", windup + recover)
172		windup_ticks_remaining = windup
173	
174		# Zero-windup attacks (weak) swing instantly this tick.
175		if windup == 0:
176			swing_this_tick = true
177		_update_sprite()
178	
179	
180	func _begin_dodge() -> void:
181		set_state(&"dodging", Balance.dodge_invincibility + Balance.dodge_recover)
182		dodge_invincible = true
183		dodge_cooldown_ticks = Balance.dodge_cooldown
184		_update_sprite()
185	
186	
187	func is_active_block() -> bool:
188		return state == &"blocking" and blocking_ticks_elapsed < Balance.block_active_ticks
189	
190	
191	func active_block_ticks_remaining() -> int:
192		if state != &"blocking":
193			return 0
194		return maxi(0, Balance.block_active_ticks - blocking_ticks_elapsed)
195	
196	
197	# --- Sprite helpers ------------------------------------------------------
198	
199	func _current_visual_stance() -> StringName:
200		match state:
201			&"attacking_strong":
202				return &"attacking_strong_windup" if windup_ticks_remaining > 0 else &"attacking_strong_recovery"
203			&"blocking":
204				return &"blocking_active" if is_active_block() else &"blocking_passive"
205			_:
206				return state
207	
208	
209	func _update_sprite() -> void:
210		var idx: int = SPRITE_INDEX.get(_current_visual_stance(), 0)
211		var rect := Rect2(idx * SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, 0,
212				SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.y)
213		var mod: Color = SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE if hit_flash_ticks > 0 else Color.WHITE
214		sprite.region_rect = rect
215		sprite.modulate = mod
216		weapon_sprite.region_rect = rect
217		weapon_sprite.modulate = mod
218

1	class_name Enemy
2	extends Combatant
3	
4	# Abstract enemy. Concrete subclasses must override _pick_attack and
5	# _pick_idle_duration. Owns enemy FSM transitions (idle -> threat -> recovery
6	# -> idle, plus exhausted side branch). Swing event happens on the last tick
7	# of threat_* — fight.gd reads `swing_this_tick` & `pending_attack_kind`.
8	
9	# --- Sprite rendering ----------------------------------------------------
10	# Enemy body = Sprite2D with region_rect cell-picker. Each FSM stance
11	# maps 1:1 to a cell in the strip (no sub-stances for basic enemy). A second
12	# Sprite2D (weapon) overlays the body using the same cell index. These
13	# consts live in the base class for this iteration because we only have one
14	# enemy type (enemy_basic). If a future enemy type wants its own sheets, move
15	# these into the subclass and let base expose abstract accessors.
16	# See sprites/SPRITES.md for the per-cell stance spec.
17	const SPRITE_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/enemy_basic.png")
18	const WEAPON_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/enemy_basic_weapon.png")
19	const SPRITE_CELL_SIZE := Vector2i(32, 32)
20	const SPRITE_SCALE := 8.0
21	const SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE := Color(2.5, 2.5, 2.5, 1.0)
22	const SPRITE_INDEX := {
23		&"idle": 0,
24		&"threat_weak": 1,
25		&"threat_strong": 2,
26		&"recovery_weak": 3,
27		&"recovery_strong": 4,
28		&"exhausted": 5,
29	}
30	
31	# Floating HP + fatigue bars above the body. Drawn here in _draw() (the sprite
32	# child node draws separately and sits visually below — bars are above body
33	# anyway at y_offset=-82/-70 so there's no overlap).
34	const HP_BAR_SIZE := Vector2(120, 10)
35	const HP_BAR_OFFSET_Y := -150.0  # bar top-left.y, relative to enemy center
36	const FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE := Vector2(120, 6)
37	const FATIGUE_BAR_OFFSET_Y := -138.0  # sits 2 px below HP bar
38	const COLOR_BAR_BG := Color(0.15, 0.15, 0.2, 1.0)
39	const COLOR_HP_BAR_FILL := Color(0.85, 0.2, 0.2, 1.0)
40	const COLOR_FATIGUE_BAR_FILL := Color(0.95, 0.85, 0.25, 1.0)
41	
42	var rng := RandomNumberGenerator.new()
43	var sprite: Sprite2D
44	var weapon_sprite: Sprite2D
45	
46	# Set true for exactly one tick when threat phase resolves into a swing.
47	var swing_this_tick: bool = false
48	# Which attack was telegraphed — &"weak" or &"strong" — read on swing tick.
49	var pending_attack_kind: StringName = &"weak"
50	
51	
52	func _ready() -> void:
53		rng.randomize()
54		sprite = _make_sprite(SPRITE_SHEET)
55		add_child(sprite)
56		# weapon_sprite added after body → drawn on top in same _draw pass.
57		weapon_sprite = _make_sprite(WEAPON_SHEET)
58		add_child(weapon_sprite)
59		# Start idle.
60		_enter_idle()
61	
62	
63	func _make_sprite(sheet: Texture2D) -> Sprite2D:
64		var s := Sprite2D.new()
65		s.texture = sheet
66		s.region_enabled = true
67		s.region_rect = Rect2(0, 0, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.y)
68		s.scale = Vector2(SPRITE_SCALE, SPRITE_SCALE)
69		s.texture_filter = CanvasItem.TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST
70		return s
71	
72	
73	func _draw() -> void:
74		# Floating HP bar above the enemy body.
75		var hp_origin := Vector2(-HP_BAR_SIZE.x * 0.5, HP_BAR_OFFSET_Y)
76		draw_rect(Rect2(hp_origin, HP_BAR_SIZE), COLOR_BAR_BG)
77		var hp_pct: float = float(hp) / float(Balance.hp_max)
78		draw_rect(Rect2(hp_origin, Vector2(HP_BAR_SIZE.x * hp_pct, HP_BAR_SIZE.y)),
79				COLOR_HP_BAR_FILL)
80	
81		# Floating fatigue bar, just below the HP bar.
82		var fat_origin := Vector2(-FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE.x * 0.5, FATIGUE_BAR_OFFSET_Y)
83		draw_rect(Rect2(fat_origin, FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE), COLOR_BAR_BG)
84		var fat_pct: float = float(fatigue) / float(Balance.fatigue_max)
85		draw_rect(Rect2(fat_origin, Vector2(FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE.x * fat_pct, FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE.y)),
86				COLOR_FATIGUE_BAR_FILL)
87	
88	
89	func tick() -> void:
90		swing_this_tick = false
91		super.tick()
92		if state_expired_this_tick:
93			_advance_state_after_expiry()
94		elif (state == &"threat_weak" or state == &"threat_strong") and state_ticks_remaining == 0:
95			# Defensive: if countdown landed exactly on zero before expiry flag
96			# (shouldn't happen since tick() sets the flag, but keep safe).
97			swing_this_tick = true
98		_update_sprite()
99	
100	
101	func _advance_state_after_expiry() -> void:
102		# Called the same tick the previous state expired.
103		match state:
104			&"idle":
105				_decide_attack()
106			&"threat_weak":
107				# Last tick of threat = swing event.
108				swing_this_tick = true
109				set_state(&"recovery_weak", _recovery_weak_ticks())
110			&"threat_strong":
111				swing_this_tick = true
112				set_state(&"recovery_strong", _recovery_strong_ticks())
113			&"recovery_weak", &"recovery_strong":
114				_enter_idle()
115			&"exhausted":
116				fatigue = Balance.fatigue_max
117				_enter_idle()
118			_:
119				_enter_idle()
120	
121	
122	func _enter_idle() -> void:
123		set_state(&"idle", _pick_idle_duration(rng))
124		pending_attack_kind = &"weak"
125	
126	
127	func _decide_attack() -> void:
128		pending_attack_kind = _pick_attack(rng)
129		if pending_attack_kind == &"strong":
130			var t: int = _threat_strong_ticks()
131			set_state(&"threat_strong", t)
132		else:
133			var tw: int = _threat_weak_ticks()
134			set_state(&"threat_weak", tw)
135	
136	
137	# Called by fight.gd when fatigue drops to 0 outside of exhausted.
138	func enter_exhausted() -> void:
139		set_state(&"exhausted", _exhausted_ticks())
140	
141	
142	# --- Sprite helpers ------------------------------------------------------
143	
144	func _update_sprite() -> void:
145		# Enemy has no sub-stances — state maps 1:1 to a cell index.
146		var idx: int = SPRITE_INDEX.get(state, 0)
147		var rect := Rect2(idx * SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, 0,
148				SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.y)
149		var mod: Color = SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE if hit_flash_ticks > 0 else Color.WHITE
150		sprite.region_rect = rect
151		sprite.modulate = mod
152		weapon_sprite.region_rect = rect
153		weapon_sprite.modulate = mod
154	
155	
156	# --- Abstract hooks ------------------------------------------------------
157	
158	func _pick_attack(_rng: RandomNumberGenerator) -> StringName:
159		# Override in subclass.
160		return &"weak"
161	
162	
163	func _pick_idle_duration(_rng: RandomNumberGenerator) -> int:
164		# Override in subclass.
165		return 48
166	
167	
168	# --- Tunable accessors (overridable) -------------------------------------
169	
170	func _threat_weak_ticks() -> int:
171		return 24
172	
173	
174	func _threat_strong_ticks() -> int:
175		return 32
176	
177	
178	func _recovery_weak_ticks() -> int:
179		return 8
180	
181	
182	func _recovery_strong_ticks() -> int:
183		return 16
184	
185	
186	func _exhausted_ticks() -> int:
187		return 64
188

4	# Per-type tunables (threat/recovery/idle/probability) live in `balance.json`
5	# under the `enemy_basic` section, accessed via the `Balance` autoload.

7	
8	func _pick_attack(rng_: RandomNumberGenerator) -> StringName:

1	class_name Combatant
2	extends Node2D
3	
4	# Base stateful entity. Holds hp, fatigue, current state and remaining ticks.
5	# Subclasses (Player, Enemy) decide transitions; this base only owns the
6	# countdown and getters/setters.
7	
8	var hp: int = Balance.hp_max
9	var fatigue: int = Balance.fatigue_max
10	var state: StringName = &"idle"
11	var state_ticks_remaining: int = 0
12	
13	# True for exactly one tick when state transitions on its own due to countdown.
14	# Subclasses can also set their own "swing_this_tick" flag for tick-precise events.
15	var state_expired_this_tick: bool = false
16	
17	# Polish: white-flash overlay when this combatant gets hit. Ticked down here;
18	# subclass _draw() overrides body colour while > 0.
19	var hit_flash_ticks: int = 0
20	
21	
22	func set_state(new_state: StringName, ticks: int) -> void:
23		state = new_state
24		state_ticks_remaining = ticks
25	
26	
27	func trigger_flash(ticks: int) -> void:
28		hit_flash_ticks = ticks
29		queue_redraw()
30	
31	
32	func tick() -> void:
33		state_expired_this_tick = false
34		if hit_flash_ticks > 0:
35			hit_flash_ticks -= 1
36			queue_redraw()  # repaint as flash starts and again when it ends
37		if state_ticks_remaining > 0:
38			state_ticks_remaining -= 1
39			if state_ticks_remaining <= 0:
40				state_expired_this_tick = true
41	
42	
43	func damage_hp(amount: int) -> void:
44		hp = clampi(hp - amount, 0, Balance.hp_max)
45		queue_redraw()
46	
47	
48	func heal_hp(amount: int) -> void:
49		hp = clampi(hp + amount, 0, Balance.hp_max)
50		queue_redraw()
51	
52	
53	func add_fatigue(amount: int) -> void:
54		fatigue = clampi(fatigue + amount, 0, Balance.fatigue_max)
55		queue_redraw()
56	
57	
58	func drain_fatigue(amount: int) -> void:
59		fatigue = clampi(fatigue - amount, 0, Balance.fatigue_max)
60		queue_redraw()
61

1	# Sprites — Stance sheet specification
2	
3	Combatanti se renderují jako jednoduchý `Sprite2D` s `region_rect` výřezem z jednoho spritesheetu na postavu. Každý FSM stance odpovídá právě jedné buňce v sheetu — žádné inter-pose animace (žádné lerpy, žádný `AnimationPlayer`). Změna stancu = swap `region_rect` na hranici ticku.
4	
5	## Globální pravidla
6	
7	- **Layout:** horizontální strip, buňky v pořadí indexu (idx 0 = nejlevější).
8	- **Pixel-perfect rendering:** `texture_filter = NEAREST` (nastaveno v `_ready()` v `player.gd` / `enemy.gd`). Žádná anti-alias, žádné mipmapy.
9	- **Display scale:** ×8 (`SPRITE_SCALE` v `player.gd` / `enemy.gd`). 32px cell → 256px na obrazovce.
10	- **Hit flash:** `sprite.modulate = Color(2.5, 2.5, 2.5, 1.0)` po dobu `hit_flash_ticks > 0`. Žádný extra draw — jen modulate boost.
11	- **Cell size:** **32×32 px** pro všechny postavy v této iteraci (player i enemy basic). Cell size je deklarován v `SPRITE_CELL_SIZE` v každém `.gd` — per-character variace zůstává možná, ale výchozí je čtvercový 32px frame.
12	- **Dummy sheety jsou vyplněné jednou barvou s 1px černým rámečkem** — rámeček je pouze pro vizuální orientaci v debug a v reálném artu nemusí (a typicky nebude) existovat. Reálný pixel-art má vyplnit celé buňky 24×N do okraje.
13	
14	## Body vs. weapon sheety
15	
16	Každý character má **dva sheety**:
17	1. `<character>.png` — tělo / postava (drawn jako spodní vrstva).
18	2. `<character>_weapon.png` — zbraň (drawn jako vrchní vrstva, **přesně stejný layout, stejný počet cell, stejné pořadí stancí**).
19	
20	Index cell se počítá jednou v `_update_sprite()` a aplikuje na oba `Sprite2D` současně — to znamená, že zbraň musí mít cell pro každý stance ve stejném pořadí jako tělo. Pokud daný stance nemá zbraň zobrazenou (např. dodging), buňka v `_weapon.png` může být čistě průhledná. Hit flash modulate se aplikuje na obě vrstvy.
21	
22	## `player.png` + `player_weapon.png` — 224×32 px (7 cells × 32×32)
23	
24	| Idx | Stance | Když se zobrazí |
25	|---:|---|---|
26	| 0 | `idle` | `state == &"idle"` |
27	| 1 | `attacking_weak` | `state == &"attacking_weak"` (instant swing — celý state) |
28	| 2 | `attacking_strong_windup` | `state == &"attacking_strong"` AND `windup_ticks_remaining > 0` |
29	| 3 | `attacking_strong_recovery` | `state == &"attacking_strong"` AND `windup_ticks_remaining == 0` |
30	| 4 | `blocking_active` | `state == &"blocking"` AND `is_active_block()` (prvních 8 ticků) |
31	| 5 | `blocking_passive` | `state == &"blocking"` po vypršení active parry window |
32	| 6 | `dodging` | `state == &"dodging"` (kryje invincibility i recovery sub-fázi) |
33	
34	Sub-stance pro player jsou odvozené v `_current_visual_stance()` v `player.gd`. FSM zná pouze 4 hlavní stavy (`idle`, `attacking_weak`, `attacking_strong`, `blocking`, `dodging`), vizuál ale rozlišuje 7 cell.
35	
36	## `enemy_basic.png` + `enemy_basic_weapon.png` — 192×32 px (6 cells × 32×32)
37	
38	| Idx | Stance | Když se zobrazí |
39	|---:|---|---|
40	| 0 | `idle` | `state == &"idle"` |
41	| 1 | `threat_weak` | `state == &"threat_weak"` (napřah ke slabému seku) |
42	| 2 | `threat_strong` | `state == &"threat_strong"` (napřah ke silnému seku — typicky červený) |
43	| 3 | `recovery_weak` | `state == &"recovery_weak"` (po seku, krátké okno na trefu od hráče) |
44	| 4 | `recovery_strong` | `state == &"recovery_strong"` (delší okno po silném seku) |
45	| 5 | `exhausted` | `state == &"exhausted"` (fatigue = 0, dlouhá obnova) |
46	
47	Enemy nemá žádné sub-stance — mapování je 1:1 `state -> idx`, takže `_current_visual_stance()` zde není potřeba (stačí `state` přímo).
48	
49	## Přidání nové postavy / stancu
50	
51	1. Vytvoř nový PNG v `res://sprites/<character>.png` jako horizontální strip s N buňkami šířky `cell_w` a výšky `cell_h`.
52	2. V příslušném `.gd` upravs konstanty:
53	   - `SPRITE_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/<character>.png")`
54	   - `SPRITE_CELL_SIZE := Vector2i(cell_w, cell_h)` — base `enemy.gd` i `player.gd` čte cell size odsud, takže per-character variace je „zadarmo“.
55	   - `SPRITE_INDEX := { &"<stance>": <idx>, ... }`
56	3. Pokud potřebuješ sub-stance (např. nový enemy s windup/recovery rozdělením), přidej v `_current_visual_stance()` match větev.
57	4. Pro nový enemy type, který chce vlastní sheet: přesuň `SPRITE_SHEET` / `SPRITE_INDEX` / `SPRITE_CELL_SIZE` consts z `enemy.gd` do podtřídy a v base ponech abstract hooky (`_sprite_sheet()`, `_sprite_cell_size()`, `_sprite_index()`).
58	
59	## Import notes
60	
61	- Godot import: `texture_filter = NEAREST` je nastaven kódem v `_ready()`, není potřeba `.import` tweak.
62	- Sheet je `Sprite2D` s `region_enabled = true`. `region_rect` se přepisuje každý tick v `_update_sprite()`.
63	- Šířka sheetu = `cell_w * count`. Výška = `cell_h`. Pokud potřebuješ vícero řádků (např. směrové varianty), bude nutné rozšířit `region_rect` cell-picker (zatím není potřeba).
64	
65	## Reminder
66	
67	- Cell size je **per-character const** v `.gd` souboru. Pokud změníš dimenze sheetu, sync `SPRITE_CELL_SIZE` v kódu.
68	- Žádné inter-pose animace. Pokud chceš animovat během stancu, je potřeba rozšířit sheet o víc buněk a animovat indexem (out of scope pro tuto iteraci).
69	- Hit flash je čistě `modulate` — žádný separate damage sprite cell.

Perfect. Now I have a complete understanding of the game. Let me synthesize a cohesive juice/feel feature package for this sprite-swap, tick-based combat system.

## Analysis

**Core strength:** Fixed 16 Hz tick loop with minimal physics and pure sprite-swap rendering means every "feel" touch is cheap and composable. The existing shake, particles, and hit flash are already there.

**Design intent:** Reaction-based: read telegraph → find opening → punish. Passivity is penalized (hits regen enemy). The game is about rhythm and timing.

**Opportunity zones for juice:**
1. **Enemy telegraph clarity** — make threat window MORE readable and punishing (add visual emphasis, maybe sound hooks).
2. **Recovery window feedback** — make the vulnerable window feel like the "time to strike" moment (visual sting, maybe a slam/clang effect).
3. **Fatigue system drama** — exhausted state is cool but feels distant; give it more arc and danger signals.
4. **Momentum/combo feel** — landing hits back-to-back should feel like the player is CONTROLLING the fight, not reactive.
5. **Block feedback** — blocking currently has no visual tick on the enemy side; the enemy should ACT like it's being resisted.

## Feature Package

I'm proposing **"Momentum & Telegraph Clarity"** — 4 features that reinforce the game's core loop:

input.theme: Make threat windows unmissable and recovery windows feel like earned punish windows; reward aggressive, rhythmic play with hit-stop and visual crescendos.

features.name: Threat Pulse — Animated cell tint on threat state
features.pitch: Enemy flashes yellow on threat onset, pulsing for 2 frames to make the telegraph window unmissable and build urgency. Creates a rhythm cue that players learn to read.
features.mechanics: New `balance.json` key `threat_pulse_color` (yellow, RGB 1.0, 0.85, 0.2). On tick boundary when enemy enters `threat_weak` or `threat_strong`, set flag `threat_pulsing` and `threat_pulse_ticks := 4` (2 frames at 16 Hz visible as bright, then fade). Modulate multiplier: `if threat_pulse_ticks > 2: modulate *= threat_pulse_color else modulate = Color.WHITE`. Decrement `threat_pulse_ticks` each tick in `enemy.gd.tick()` before state countdown. Pure visual, zero gameplay impact.
features.spriteWorkaround: Modulate tint only; reuses existing stance cells.
features.risks: Color choice must be legible on sprite art; cyan/magenta might work if yellow clashes.

features.name: Hit-Stop on Strong Hits — Freeze-frame momentum
features.pitch: Strong attacks and parry drains freeze the screen for 3 ticks, making impact feel HEAVY and giving the player a beat to enjoy the damage. Weak hits don't freeze, so weak spam feels snappy by contrast.
features.mechanics: New `balance.json` key `hit_stop_strong_ticks := 3` and `hit_stop_parry_ticks := 2`. In `fight.gd._resolve_enemy_swing()` when active parry triggers, set `game_hit_stop_ticks := hit_stop_parry_ticks`. In `_resolve_player_swing()` when strong hits land, set `game_hit_stop_ticks := hit_stop_strong_ticks`. Add `game_hit_stop_ticks: int = 0` counter in `fight.gd`. In `_on_tick()`, if `game_hit_stop_ticks > 0`, decrement but **skip the rest of the tick loop** (return early so state countdowns don't advance). Rendering still queues, so particles + flash are visible during freeze. Blocks weak attacks from registering during freeze.
features.spriteWorkaround: No art needed; it's a tick-timing freeze.
features.risks: If too long, feels sluggish; too short, unnoticed. 3 ticks = ~188 ms, feels snappy. Must not apply to weak spam or it breaks rhythm.

features.name: Recovery Window Indicator — Enemy flickers when vulnerable
features.pitch: When enemy enters `recovery_weak` or `recovery_strong`, it flickers red for 1 tick, signalling 'now is your moment to strike.' Creates a visual rhythm that rewards reading the telegraph.
features.mechanics: New flag `enemy_recovery_flash: int = 0` (ticks remaining). On tick boundary in `enemy._advance_state_after_expiry()` when transitioning INTO `recovery_weak` or `recovery_strong`, set `enemy_recovery_flash := 1`. In `_update_sprite()`, if `enemy_recovery_flash > 0`, multiply modulate by `Color(1.2, 0.7, 0.7, 1.0)` (reddish tint). Decrement `enemy_recovery_flash` at start of `enemy.tick()`. Pure polish.
features.risks: Red may overlap visually with hit flash if both trigger simultaneously. Solution: check `hit_flash_ticks` and blend, or accept the flash priority.

features.name: Exhausted Decay Pulse — Danger countdown visual
features.pitch: Enemy exhausted state pulses orange->red over its 64-tick duration, creating mounting visual pressure. In the last 8 ticks, it throbs rapidly, signalling 'you must finish this now or lose your advantage.'
features.mechanics: In `enemy.gd.tick()`, if `state == &"exhausted"`: compute pulse phase as a function of `state_ticks_remaining` (0..64). Map 64->0 to a hue/brightness curve: early exhausted is dim orange, final 16 ticks pulse orange-red 2 ticks per cycle, final 8 ticks pulse at 1 tick per cycle. Implement via `exhausted_pulse_intensity: float = 1.0 - (state_ticks_remaining / 64.0)` and `pulse_color := Color.from_hsv(0.05 + exhausted_pulse_intensity * 0.15, 0.8, 0.6 + exhausted_pulse_intensity * 0.4, 1.0)`. Blend: `if state == &"exhausted": modulate *= pulse_color`. No new balance keys; pure visual math.
features.spriteWorkaround: Pure modulate math on existing exhausted cell.
features.risks: Color math may need tuning; HSV mapping is tricky to preview. Recommend implementing and playtesting. Could be simplified to a binary pulse if HSV feels buggy.

input.cohesionRationale: These four features work together to reinforce the core reaction loop: (1) **Threat Pulse** makes the telegraph unmissable, teaching rhythm. (2) **Recovery Flash** marks the punish window so players learn when to strike. (3) **Hit-Stop on Strong** makes successful reads feel SATISFYING (freeze-frame reward), while weak spam stays snappy. (4) **Exhausted Decay Pulse** creates mounting pressure and urgency as the player approaches victory. Together they form a visual language: *bright yellow = read this*, *red flicker = strike now*, *heavy freeze = you won*, *pulsing orange-red = finish the job*. Zero new rules, zero new art, pure feel. Each can be tuned independently via balance.json or modulate math.
input.feasibilityConcerns: Hit-Stop is the most complex: requires early return logic in _on_tick() to skip state countdowns. Must be carefully ordered so swing resolution still fires (resolve before the early return, or trigger hit-stop *after* resolve). Exhausted Decay Pulse HSV math could introduce floating-point jank; recommend clamping and testing. Modulate blending order matters if multiple effects stack (hit flash + threat pulse + recovery flash); current code does not layer modulates, so conflicts must be resolved by priority (hit flash > threat pulse > recovery flash > exhausted pulse). All four features are purely visual with zero gameplay logic changes, so they don't touch FSM, balance mechanics, or damage numbers.
input.topPick: **Threat Pulse** is the top pick because it solves the highest-value problem: making the telegraph window *readable* and *teachable*. New players struggle to see when the enemy is about to attack; a single yellow flash on threat onset fixes that for free. It has the highest juice-to-effort ratio and no risk of breaking the delicate timing. Ship this first, then add Hit-Stop (strongest payoff for victory) and Recovery Flash (smallest but most satisfying). Exhausted Decay can come later as polish.

---

## Summary

I've analyzed the full Glamdring architecture and proposed **"Momentum & Telegraph Clarity"** — a 4-feature juice package that reinforces the game's core reaction loop without breaking any rules or adding art requirements.

### The Package

1. **Threat Pulse** (S effort) — Enemy flashes yellow when threat state begins, making the telegraph unmissable and teaching rhythm. Pure modulate tint.

2. **Hit-Stop on Strong Hits** (S effort) — Strong attacks and successful parries freeze the game for 3 ticks, making impact feel HEAVY and rewarding precision reads. Weak spam stays snappy by contrast.

3. **Recovery Window Indicator** (S effort) — Enemy flickers red when entering recovery states, creating a visual rhythm that tells players *when* to strike. Pure tint.

4. **Exhausted Decay Pulse** (M effort) — Enemy pulses orange→red over the exhausted duration, with final 8 ticks throbbing rapidly to create mounting pressure. Pure HSV modulate math.

### Why This Works

- **Zero new sprite art** — all modulate tints and timing tweaks.
- **No rule changes** — purely visual/feel layer on top of existing mechanics.
- **Reinforces core loop** — telegraph → read → punish is now *visually* telegraphed, making the game more learnable and satisfying.
- **Tunable** — colors, timings, and pulse curves live in balance.json or simple math, so playtesting can dial in the exact vibe.
- **Minimal risk** — each feature is isolated; they can be added/removed independently.

The **Threat Pulse** is the top pick because it solves the biggest usability problem (new players can't see the telegraph) with zero downsides, and it makes the game *rhythmic* — something the sprite-swap, tick-based architecture is perfect for expressing.
